name: Tafsir al-Mizan
author: Allamah Muhammad Husayn Tabatabai
text:
# Ayahs

```arabic
۞ وَٱتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ إِذْ قَالَ لِقَوْمِهِۦ يَـٰقَوْمِ إِن كَانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُم مَّقَامِى وَتَذْكِيرِى بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ فَعَلَى ٱللَّهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ فَأَجْمِعُوٓا۟ أَمْرَكُمْ وَشُرَكَآءَكُمْ ثُمَّ لَا يَكُنْ أَمْرُكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ غُمَّةً ثُمَّ ٱقْضُوٓا۟ إِلَىَّ وَلَا تُنظِرُونِ (٧١)
```

```english
Relate to them the account of Noah when he said to his people, ‘O my people! If my stay [among you] be hard on you and [also] my reminding you of Allah’s signs, [for my part] I have put my trust in Allah. So conspire together, along with your partners, leaving nothing vague in your plan, then carry it out against me without giving me any respite. (71)
```

```arabic
فَإِن تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَمَا سَأَلْتُكُم مِّنْ أَجْرٍ ۖ إِنْ أَجْرِىَ إِلَّا عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۖ وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ ٱلْمُسْلِمِينَ (٧٢)
```

```english
If you turn your back [on me], I do not ask any reward from you; my reward lies only with Allah and I have been commanded to be of those who submit [to Allah].’ (72)
```

```arabic
فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَنَجَّيْنَـٰهُ وَمَن مَّعَهُۥ فِى ٱلْفُلْكِ وَجَعَلْنَـٰهُمْ خَلَـٰٓئِفَ وَأَغْرَقْنَا ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِنَا ۖ فَٱنظُرْ كَيْفَ كَانَ عَـٰقِبَةُ ٱلْمُنذَرِينَ (٧٣)
```

```english
But they impugned him. So We delivered him and those who were with him in the ark and We made them the successors, and We drowned those who denied Our signs. So observe how was the fate of those who were warned! (73)
```

```arabic
ثُمَّ بَعَثْنَا مِنۢ بَعْدِهِۦ رُسُلًا إِلَىٰ قَوْمِهِمْ فَجَآءُوهُم بِٱلْبَيِّنَـٰتِ فَمَا كَانُوا۟ لِيُؤْمِنُوا۟ بِمَا كَذَّبُوا۟ بِهِۦ مِن قَبْلُ ۚ كَذَٰلِكَ نَطْبَعُ عَلَىٰ قُلُوبِ ٱلْمُعْتَدِينَ (٧٤)
```

```english
Then after him We sent [other] apostles to their people. They brought them manifest proofs, but they would not believe something they had denied before. Thus do We seal the hearts of the transgressors. (74)
```

## (Commentary)

The verses summarize the story of Noah (peace be upon him) and the messengers who followed him up to the time of Moses and Aaron (peace be upon them). They describe how Allah, the Glorified, dealt with their nations who disbelieved in their messengers—how He destroyed them and saved His messengers and those who believed in them—so that the deniers among this nation might take heed.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ** (**Relate to them the story of Noah**) to the end of the verse. The word "standing" (المقام) is a mim-infinitive (مصدر ميمي) as well as a noun of time and place derived from "standing" (القيام). What is intended here is either the first or the third meaning; that is, "my standing (قيامي) in the matter of calling to the monotheism (التوحيد) — *the absolute oneness of Allah in His essence, attributes, and acts*" — or "my station and status," which is the status of messengership. The word "resolve" (الإجماع) means determination (العزم), and it is sometimes used with the preposition "upon" (على). Al-Raghib said: "I resolved (أجمعت) such-and-such is mostly said regarding a matter that is a collection (جمع) reached through reflection, such as: 'So resolve (أجمعوا) upon your plan and [call] your partners.'"

The word "distress" (الغمة) refers to anguish and severity, and it carries the meaning of "covering" (التغطية), as if grief covers the heart. From this comes the word "clouds" (الغمام) for mist, named so because it covers the face of the sky. "Executing" (القضاء) a matter toward something means completing its affair through killing, annihilation, or the like.

The meaning of the verse is: "Relate" (وَاتْلُ), O Muhammad, "to them the story of Noah" (عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ نُوحٍ) and his great news, when he confronted his people while he was alone, speaking for himself, sent to the people of the world. He challenged them to do to him whatever they wished if they were able, and he established the proof against his deniers in that regard: "when he said to his people, 'O my people! If my standing'" (إِذْ قالَ لِقَوْمِهِ يا قَوْمِ إِنْ كانَ كَبُرَ عَلَيْكُمْ مَقامِي) — meaning my rising for the matter of calling to monotheism or my station of messengership — "'and my reminding [you] of Allah’s signs'" (وَتَذْكِيرِي بِآياتِ اللهِ) has become burdensome for you, and it inevitably drives you to kill me and inflict whatever evil you can upon me to relieve yourselves of me, "'then I have put my trust in Allah'" (فَعَلَى اللهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ). This is in response to the threat posed by the tightening of your chests and the narrowness of your souls toward me, by returning my affair to Him and making Him a trustee (وكيل) who manages my affairs without me occupying myself with planning. "'So resolve upon your plan and [call] your partners'" (فأَجْمِعُوا أَمْرَكُمْ وَشُرَكاءَكُمْ) whom you claim will help you in hardships, and determine against me whatever occurs to you—this is a command of incapacitation (تعجيزي). "'Then do not let your plan be a source of distress to you'" (ثُمَّ لا يَكُنْ أَمْرُكُمْ عَلَيْكُمْ غُمَّةً) if you have not exerted yourselves in seeking every means to repel me. "'Then execute [it] against me'" (ثُمَّ اقْضُوا إِلَيَ) by repelling and killing me, "'and do not give me any respite'" (وَلا تُنْظِرُونِ) and do not delay me.

In the verse is his (peace be upon him) challenge to his people to do to him whatever they wished, and a demonstration that his Lord is powerful (قدير) enough to repel them from him, even if they unite against him and seek help from their partners and deities.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَإِنْ تَوَلَّيْتُمْ فَما سَأَلْتُكُمْ مِنْ أَجْرٍ** (**But if you turn your backs, I have not asked any reward of you**) to the end of the verse. This is a derivation from his trust in his Lord. His saying: "I have not asked you" (فَما سَأَلْتُكُمْ) etc., is like placing the cause in the position of the effect. The implied meaning is: "If you turn away and refuse to respond to my call, there is no harm to me in that, for I am not harmed by your turning away in the least. I would only have been harmed by your turning away from me if I had asked you for a reward for that, which would be lost by your turning away. I have asked you for no reward; my reward is only with Allah."

His saying: **وَأُمِرْتُ أَنْ أَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ** (**and I have been commanded to be among those who submit**) means those who submit (يسلمون) the affair to Him in what He wills for them or against them. They do not wax proud against His command by submitting to other apparent causes such that they would humble themselves before them or expect them to bring benefit or repel harm.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَنَجَّيْناهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ وَجَعَلْناهُمْ خَلائِفَ** (**But they impugned him, so We delivered him and those who were with him in the ark, and We made them successors**) to the end of the verse. The "successors" (الخلائف) is the plural of "successor" (خليفة); meaning, We made these survivors successors in the earth, and those who remained after them would succeed their predecessors and take their place. The rest is clear.

**His saying, the Exalted:** **ثُمَّ بَعَثْنا مِنْ بَعْدِهِ رُسُلاً إِلى قَوْمِهِمْ** (**Then, after him, We sent messengers to their people**) to the end of the verse. By "messengers," He means those who came after Noah until the time of Moses (peace be upon him). The apparent context suggests that "clear proofs" (البينات) refers to the miraculous signs (الآيات المعجزة) that the nations demanded from their prophets after their arrival, their call, and the nations' denial of them. So they brought them, and therein was the judgment between them and their nations. This is supported by His subsequent saying: **فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ** (**But they would not believe in what they had impugned before**). What first comes to mind is that they brought them clear signs, but Allah had already sealed their hearts because of their transgression, so it was not within their power to believe a second time in what they had denied the first time.

The necessary implication of this is that their denial occurred before the messengers brought those clear signs. The messengers had already spread their call among them and invited them to the monotheism of Allah, but they denied it and them; then they demanded a miraculous sign, and when they brought it to them, they still did not believe.

We have already presented some discussion on this verse in the commentary on His saying: **فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا مِنْ قَبْلُ** (**But they would not believe in what they had impugned before**) — *al-A'raf: 101* — in the eighth volume of this book. We explained there that the verse contains an allusion to the primordial realm (عالم الذر) — *the state of human existence prior to this world where souls testified to Allah's Lordship* — though this does not contradict its yielding the meaning we just mentioned; so let it be referred to.

## (Narrative Discussion)

In *al-Kafi*, narrated from Muhammad ibn Yahya, from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn, from Muhammad ibn Isma'il, from Salih ibn 'Uqbah, from 'Abdullah ibn Muhammad al-Ju'fi and 'Uqbah, both from Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him), who said: **إن الله عز وجل خلق الخلق ـ فخلق من أحب مما أحب ـ فكان مما أحب أن خلقه من طين الجنة ـ وخلق من أبغض مما أبغض ـ وكان ما أبغضه أن خلقه من طينة النار ـ ثم بعثهم في الظلال ، فقلت : وأي شيء الظلال؟ فقال : ألم تر إلى ظلك في الشمس شيء وليس بشيء ـ. ثم بعث منهم النبيين ـ فدعوهم إلى الإقرار بالله عز وجل : «وَلَئِنْ سَأَلْتَهُمْ مَنْ خَلَقَهُمْ لَيَقُولُنَّ اللهُ» ثم دعوهم إلى الإقرار بالنبيين ـ فأقر بعض وأنكر بعض ، ثم دعوهم إلى ولايتنا فأقر بها والله من أحب ـ وأنكرها من أبغض ، وهو قوله : «فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ». ثم قال أبو جعفر عليه‌السلام : كان التكذيب من قبل.** — "Indeed, Allah, Mighty and Majestic, created the creation. He created those He loved from what He loved, and what He loved was that He created them from the clay of Paradise. And He created those He hated from what He hated, and what He hated was that He created them from the clay of the Fire. Then He raised them in the shadows (الظلال)." I asked: "And what are the shadows?" He replied: "Have you not seen your shadow in the sun? It is something, yet it is nothing. Then He raised the prophets from among them and they called them to the acknowledgment of Allah, Mighty and Majestic: 'And if you ask them who created them, they will surely say, "Allah."' Then they called them to the acknowledgment of the prophets; some acknowledged and some denied. Then they called them to our guardianship (الولاية), and by Allah, those He loved acknowledged it, and those He hated denied it. And that is His saying: 'But they would not believe in what they had impugned before.'" Then Abu Ja'far (peace be upon him) said: "The denial was from before."

**I say:** It was also narrated in *al-'Ilal* with its chain to Muhammad ibn Isma'il from Salih from 'Abdullah and 'Uqbah from him (peace be upon him), and al-'Ayyashi narrated it from al-Ju'fi from him (peace be upon him).

In the *Tafsir* of al-'Ayyashi, narrated from Zurarah and Humran from Abu Ja'far and Abu 'Abdillah (peace be upon them): **خلق الخلق وهم أظلة ـ فأرسل رسوله محمدا صلى‌الله‌عليه‌وآله ـ فمنهم من آمن به ومنهم من كذبه ـ ثم بعثه في الخلق الآخر فآمن به من كان آمن به في الأظلة ـ وجحده من جحده يومئذ فقال : «فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ».** — "He created the creation while they were shadows (أظلة). Then He sent His Messenger Muhammad (peace be upon him and his progeny); some of them believed in him and some denied him. Then He raised him in the other creation, and those who had believed in him in the shadows believed in him [again], and those who had rejected him then rejected him [again]. Thus He said: 'But they would not believe in what they had impugned before.'"

**I say:** We have detailed the discussion on what is called the primordial realm (عالم الذر) in the commentary on His saying, the Exalted: **وَإِذْ أَخَذَ رَبُّكَ مِنْ بَنِي آدَمَ مِنْ ظُهُورِهِمْ ذُرِّيَّتَهُمْ وَأَشْهَدَهُمْ عَلى أَنْفُسِهِمْ أَلَسْتُ بِرَبِّكُمْ قالُوا بَلى** (**When your Lord took from the Children of Adam, from their loins, their descendants and made them bear witness over themselves, [saying to them,] ‘Am I not your Lord?’ They said, ‘Yes indeed!’**). We clarified there that the verses of the primordial realm establish another human world distinct from this material (مادي), gradual (تدريجي) human world that is mixed with pains, calamities, disobediences, and sins, which we witness through the senses.

It is concurrent with this sensible world in a certain way, but it is not governed by these material laws. Its precedence over this world of ours is not a temporal (بالزمان) precedence, but rather another kind of precedence, similar to the precedence understood from His saying: **أَنْ يَقُولَ لَهُ كُنْ فَيَكُونُ** (**[His command, when He wills anything, is only to] say to it, ‘Be!’ and it is**) — *Ya-Sin: 82*. For "Be" (كن) and "it is" (يكون) describe a single referent (مصداق), which is the external existence (وجود) of the thing. However, this very existence, in its aspect directed toward Allah, precedes itself in its other aspect. In its divine aspect, it is neither gradual nor temporal, nor is it absent from its Lord or severed from Him, unlike its aspect directed toward the creation, according to the detail provided there.

The narrations we have cited in this traditionary discourse point to the primordial realm like those that passed previously, but they possess a unique merit: the subtle expression "the shadows" (الظلال). Through profound contemplation of this expression, the intended meaning becomes clear in the best possible way. For within cosmic things, there are matters that are like shadows in that they are inseparable from them, reflecting the specificities of their existence and the effects of their existence; yet, they are they, and they are not they.

For if we look at things and strip our gaze, focusing solely on their being the pure handiwork and act of Allah—not detached from Him nor separated from Him—which is a true and factual perspective, nothing is realized in them except submission (التسليم) to Allah, humility before His will, lowliness before His majesty, attachment to His mercy and the matter of His Lordship, and faith in His oneness and in what He sent His messengers with and revealed to them of His religion.

These existences (وجودات) are shadows—things and yet not things—when compared to the existences of material things, taking the material world as the primary point of comparison. This is what the verses are built upon, as their purpose is to clarify the establishment of the obligation (التكليف) of monotheism as an inescapable obligation for which one will be questioned on the Day of Resurrection.

However, if the aspect of the Lord, the Exalted, were taken as the primary point and this material world, with its material existents, were compared to it—which is also a true perspective—then this world would be the shadow, and the aspect of the Lord, the Exalted, would be the origin and the person to whom the shadow belongs, as alluded to by His saying, the Exalted: **كُلُّ شَيْءٍ هالِكٌ إِلَّا وَجْهَهُ** (**Everything is to perish except His Face**) — *al-Qasas: 88* — and His saying: **كُلُّ مَنْ عَلَيْها فانٍ وَيَبْقى وَجْهُ رَبِّكَ** (**Everyone on it is ephemeral, yet there lasts the Face of your Lord**) — *al-Rahman: 26-27*.

As for what al-Ayyashi narrated from Abu Basir from Abu 'Abdillah (peace be upon him) regarding His saying: **فَما كانُوا لِيُؤْمِنُوا بِما كَذَّبُوا بِهِ مِنْ قَبْلُ** (**But they would not believe in what they had impugned before**), he said: **بعث الله الرسل إلى الخلق ـ وهم في أصلاب الرجال وأرحام النساء ـ فمن صدق حينئذ صدق بعد ذلك ، ومن كذب حينئذ كذب بعد ذلك** — "Allah sent the messengers to the creation while they were in the loins of men and the wombs of women. Whoever believed then, believed thereafter; and whoever denied then, denied thereafter." Its apparent meaning is that the sending of messengers related to the sperm-drops (النطف) in the loins and wombs, and that they were living, rational, and legally responsible (مكلفون). This is something rejected by necessity (الضرورة), as previously mentioned in the discussion on the verse of the primordial realm—unless it is interpreted to mean that the primordial realm encompasses this material, gradual, temporal world by virtue of it being non-temporal (غير زماني), such that the primordial existence (الوجود الذري) does not relate to one time over another. However, this remains a remote interpretation.
